meta_description: We carry two brass alloys: C260 and C360. Brass is a weather and corrosion resistant metal with tensile strength similar to mild steel. It's also an easy to machine material.

sections.heading: Brass CNC Machining
sections.text: Brass CNC Machining Well rounded properties make brass a versatile metal for CNC machined components. Get Brass Parts

sections.heading: What is Brass?
sections.text: What is Brass? Brass is an alloying consisting of both copper and zinc. The gold material is a weather- and corrosion- resistant metal with tensile strength similar to mild steel. It's also an easy to machine material, so feedrates can remain high and coolant need is minimal.

sections.heading: Types of Brass for CNC Machining
sections.text: Types of Brass for CNC Machining Brass C260 Brass C260, or cartridge brass, contains 70 percent copper and 30 percent zinc. The brass alloy has high ductility and is considered the most general purpose of all brass alloys. Brass C360 Brass C360 remains the industry standard for general machining and high volume production. The excellent machinability characteristic of C360 permits full utilization of a screw machine’s capabilities.

sections.heading: Brass Machining Applications
sections.text: Brass Machining Applications Automotive terminals Handles and hinges Jewelry Screws and rivets Valve stems Musical instruments Plumbing components

sections.heading: Material Properties for Machined Brass

CNC Machining Design Guide In this guide to CNC machining, you'll learn how to create efficient, manufacturable metal and plastic parts for prototyping and production.
